reasoning: Soup Hoành Thánh translates to Wonton Soup, matching description D which mentions shrimp, pork dumplings, lettuce, onion and scallions in chicken broth.
Chim Cút Rôti means Roasted Quail - Chim Cút is quail and Rôti means roasted.
Bánh Oai Vạc Chiên Hoặc Hấp refers to dumplings that are fried or steamed, matching Saigon Ravioli description with chicken, pork, and vegetables.
Bánh Bột Chiên Hành means fried flour/rice cakes with scallions (Hành), matching Scallions Pancake.
Bún Xào Đơ Biển is vermicelli stir-fried with seafood, matching the vermicelli with seafood sautéed with lemon grass sauce.
Bánh Xèo is the traditional Vietnamese Crepe made with rice powder pancake filled with shrimp, pork, and bean sprouts.
Gỏi Tôm Hoặc Gỏi Gà means salad with shrimp or chicken, matching Vietnamese salad with choice of poached shrimp, chicken, or combination.
Gỏi Ngò Sen translates to Lotus Stem Salad.
Phở Hoặc Mì Gà is noodle soup (Phở or Mì) with chicken (Gà), matching the noodle soup with shredded chicken. This is one of the two dishes from the Famous Vietnamese Noodle Soup section.
Bò Xào Cà Ry means beef (Bò) sautéed in curry (Cà Ry).
Bún Bò Huế is the famous Huế-style spicy beef noodle soup with lemon grass.
Thịt Lợi (likely Thịt Lợn meaning pork) matches Teriyaki with choice of meat.
Bún Thịt Nướng means vermicelli with grilled meat, matching vermicelli with choice of grilled meat.
Bún Chả Giò means vermicelli with crispy spring rolls (Chả Giò).
Chả Giò are Crispy Spring Rolls - a savory mixture wrapped in spring roll and fried golden brown.
Bún Thịt Nướng Chả Giò combines vermicelli with grilled meat and spring rolls.
Bún Tôm means vermicelli with shrimp (Tôm), matching vermicelli with grilled shrimp.
Bún Tôm Thịt Nướng Chả Giò combines vermicelli with shrimp, grilled meat, and spring rolls.
Cá Salmon Hoặc Cá Bông Lau Hấp means salmon or catfish (Cá Bông Lau) steamed (Hấp).
Mì Xào means stir-fried yellow noodle (Mì), matching sautéed crispy yellow noodle.
